76228.subject: Potvrdenie rezervácie - NOVA SPA Premium Spa&Wellness
76228.body: Bookio. Dobrý deň p. Novosadov Oleh, Potvrdzujeme týmto Vašu rezerváciu v NOVA SPA Premium Spa&Wellness . Je oproti bežnému jednorazovému vstupu o 50% lacnejšia, prenosná a teda použiteľná pre celú rodinu alebo kolektív, platná celých 365 dní od aktivácie. Dostupné aj online. Pri kúpe naživo aj s bonusom a krásnym balením. Ako jediný košický prémiový wellness sme tu pre Vás 360 dní v roku, 7 dní v týždni, od 9:00 do 21:00 v 100% kvalite. Váš tím NOVA SPA. Ak chcete zrušiť Vašu rezerváciu, kliknete Zrušiť rezerváciu Kde nás nájdete? 76226.subject: Hyperliquid Opens the Gates
76226.body:
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/eaa70932-7ce4-467e-b338-a662ed575d6a/0xR_NL_Banner_Heading_Wordmark.jpg?t=1776775276)
Caption: 

———————————————————————————

Happy Monday!

Markets diverged sharply last week as BTC gained 1.4% while equities, gold, and the broader AI trade sold off. The move offered an early indication that BTC may be regaining relative strength, although modest ETF inflows suggest institutional demand has yet to confirm the shift. Within crypto, liquidity rotated back toward Solana, led by PUMP and a broader rebound across ecosystem tokens.

We then examine Hyperliquid’s plan to make HIP-4 deployment permissionless. The key detail is the initial allocation of 100 outcomes per deployer, which materially lowers the marginal cost of launching markets after the initial stake and could accelerate growth from HIP-4’s currently muted base.

———————————————————————————

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/42e942ec-f31d-4957-8722-a3ac7b121e1e/0xR_NL_Market_Update.png?t=1767631151) [Market Update]
Caption: 

Last week saw a sharp divergence across major asset classes. BTC was the only major benchmark to finish the week in the green, rising 1.4%, while the Nasdaq, S&P 500 and Gold fell -3.3%, -1.2% and -2.1% respectively. If sustained, this would mark a notable shift after BTC broadly underperformed traditional risk assets over the past year.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/8af2ff42-c898-4e5b-8e30-d86e94dc2c7f/image.png?t=1784547980)
Caption: 

The weakness in equities was concentrated in the AI trade. Hot names such as Marvell (-20%), IBM (-26.9%), Super Micro Computer (-13.8%), Sandisk (-24.5%) and KOSPI (-12%) all sold off sharply. Investors have begun [questioning](https://www.reuters.com/world/asia-pacific/chipmakers-other-high-flying-stocks-slide-ai-trade-wobbles-2026-07-17/) whether the massive AI infrastructure spending can generate adequate returns, while concerns around cheaper Chinese models and delayed product launches [triggered](https://www.reuters.com/world/asia-pacific/chipmakers-other-high-flying-stocks-slide-ai-trade-wobbles-2026-07-17/) profit-taking that was amplified by leveraged positioning.

BTC, meanwhile, showed signs of decoupling. Part of the strength may reflect capital rotating out of crowded AI positions into sectors that have significantly underperformed, with BTC still down more than 45% over the past year. That said, institutional demand remains modest. Spot BTC ETFs recorded just $22M of net inflows last week, making ETF flows the key metric to watch if this relative strength is to continue.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/upload

76225.subject: Bybit Galaxy: A guide to Bybit's loyalty program
76225.body:
Bybit Learn

Автор: Bybit


Автор: Bybit

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/bybit-galaxy-guide-bybits-loyalty-program-bybitexchange-p2yze?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BjUTK8D5vQ0yqd5A%2B4I2baw%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=2xkMHFwtM2RIk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mrta9ad9~z4-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mrta9ad9-z4
  
Bybit Galaxy is a loyalty program that rewards you for actively using multiple products on the Bybit platform. Rather than completing one-off tasks for a seasonal campaign, Galaxy lets you earn points through real trading activity and redeem them for rewards on a rolling basis. This guide explains what Galaxy is, how the points system works and how reward pools let you turn consistent activity into tangible prizes. Key Takeaways: Bybit Galaxy rewards you with points for trading designated pairs and completing daily tasks across multiple Bybit products. Points expire after a certain period, encouraging consistent daily engagement rather than one-time activity. You spend accumulated points to claim rewards from limited-time reward pools on a first-come, first-served basis. What is Bybit Galaxy? Bybit Galaxy is an always-on loyalty program designed to reward cumulative transactors. That means users who don't just trade once and leave, but who engage with multiple Bybit products on an ongoing basis. The program creates a persistent loop: trade and use products to earn points, accumulate enough points to become eligible for a reward pool, claim your reward and then reinvest. Each cycle encourages you to stay active across the platform. Unlike programs focused solely on speculative token airdrops, Galaxy offers a broader range of rewards including USDT, RWA tokens and project tokens. How does Bybit Galaxy work? The program operates in three stages: 1. Earn points. Trade designated Galaxy pairs on Spot or Futures markets, or complete daily tasks using other Bybit products such as TradFi, Alpha, Fiat, Card/Pay, Earn and Loan. 2. Maintain your balance. Points expire after a certain period. You need to stay active to keep your point count high enough for upcoming reward pools. 3. Claim rewards. When a reward pool goes live and you meet the eligibility threshold, you can claim on a first-come, first-served basis. The points formula is straightforward: for every $2 in trading volume or product activity, you earn 1 point. The calculation scales logarithmically, so larger volumes earn proportionally fewer points per dollar. For example, $8 in trading volume earns you 3 points, not 4. Because the points formula scales logarithmically, spreading your activity across multiple eligible products is generally more efficient than concentrating it in a single one. For example,

76224.subject: Nvidia's tiny model beat everyone
76224.body:
The Atlas

Автор: GenAI Works


Автор: GenAI Works

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/nvidias-tiny-model-beat-everyone-genai-works-eteqe?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3Bxvgk%2FdAhS4evQWkt4HPPFA%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=3j2apBL_E1RIk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mrta3480~52-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mrta3480-52
  
Welcome back! a 4-bit retriever from NVIDIA that barely gives up any quality, two open giants from China aiming straight at the closed frontier and a breach that shows why running your own model locally just became security hygiene. 4 topics, one takeaway: owning your model now beats renting it, on quality, cost and security.  In today's Atlas Newsletter: NVIDIA's Nemotron 3 Embed tops the retrieval leaderboard, and its 4-bit variant halves memory for a third of a point Alibaba's Qwen3.8-Max calls itself "second only to Fable 5" and promises open weights soon Kimi K3 becomes the first open model past 3 trillion parameters Hugging Face gets breached by an autonomous AI agent, then cracks the case with AI it had to run in house PS: Want to collaborate with the biggest GTM engine and distribution moat for AI companies? You can feature your tool with us and reach millions. NVIDIA's #1 embedding model has a 4-bit variant that keeps 99.5% of the quality at half the memory NVIDIA's Nemotron 3 Embed family topped the RTEB retrieval leaderboard. The NVFP4 variant is the technically interesting piece: it shows how far you can compress a retriever before quality moves. The base 1B model is a Ministral-3-3B backbone converted from causal decoder to bidirectional encoder, which matters for retrieval since every token attends to full context.  It uses mean pooling to produce a 2048-dim embedding, handles 32k sequences across 34 languages and supports Matryoshka slicing:  keep the first 1024 or 512 dims and re-normalize to trade vector size for storage without retraining. The NVFP4 checkpoint quantizes weights and activations of linear layers to 4-bit via Model Optimizer.  Post-training quantization alone hurts long-context accuracy, so NVIDIA added Quantization-Aware Distillation to recover it.  The result: BF16 scores 72.38 on RTEB, NVFP4 scores 72.00. A 0.38-point drop for up to 2x the throughput on Blackwell. The deployment notes read like a real production model.  It's vLLM-only, pinned to 0.25.0, with 0.23.x and 0.24.x flagged as broken.  The recommended /v2/embed endpoint applies query and document prompt metadata via an input_type field, so you send raw strings.  The one setting most teams get wrong is CUDA graph capture size, which drives both cold-start time and memory, so NVIDIA recommends 4096 for autoscaling services and a sparse workload-aligned set beyond
